channel.title: Religion on Shit PH Said, Mostly By Anwar
channel.description: Recent content in Religion on Shit PH Said, Mostly By Anwar

item.title: [BM] Bukan Islam Biayai JAKIM RM2.6B Melalui Cukai — 'Jangan Hina Kami Sebagai Pendatang'
item.description:
Said by: Non-Muslim taxpayers / Civil Society

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-10-15

Peruntukan hal ehwal Islam RM2.6B Bajet 2026 mencetuskan perdebatan. Bukan Islam berkata mereka menerima pembiayaan hal ehwal Islam melalui cukai tetapi merayu: ‘jangan hina kami sebagai pendatang dan kafir.’ Kementerian Perpaduan mendapat RM711J berbanding JAKIM RM2B+.

item.title: [BM] Bukan Islam Danai RM2.6B JAKIM Melalui Cukai — 'Jangan Hina Kami Sebagai Pendatang'
item.description:
Said by: Non-Muslim taxpayers / Civil Society

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-10-15

RM2.6B Budget 2026 Islamic affairs allocation sparked debate. Non-Muslims said they accept funding Islamic affairs through taxes but pleaded: ‘don’t insult us as pendatang and kafir.’ Unity Ministry got RM711M vs JAKIM’s RM2B+.

item.title: Non-Muslims Fund RM2.6B JAKIM Through Taxes — 'Don't Insult Us as Pendatang'
item.description:
Said by: Non-Muslim taxpayers / Civil Society

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-10-15

RM2.6B Budget 2026 Islamic affairs allocation sparked debate. Non-Muslims said they accept funding Islamic affairs through taxes but pleaded: ‘don’t insult us as pendatang and kafir.’ Unity Ministry got RM711M vs JAKIM’s RM2B+.

item.title: [BM] Bajet JAKIM Catat Rekod RM2.6 Bilion — Tertinggi Dalam Sejarah Malaysia
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Finance Minister

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-10-11

Budget 2026 allocated RM2.6B for Islamic affairs — the highest in Malaysian history. Trajectory: RM1.9B (2024), RM2B (2025), RM2.6B (2026). Unity Ministry got only RM711M by comparison.

item.title: [BM] Bajet JAKIM Cecah Rekod RM2.6 Bilion — Tertinggi Dalam Sejarah Malaysia
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Finance Minister

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-10-11

Bajet 2026 memperuntukkan RM2.6B untuk hal ehwal Islam — tertinggi dalam sejarah Malaysia. Trajektori: RM1.9B (2024), RM2B (2025), RM2.6B (2026). Kementerian Perpaduan hanya mendapat RM711J sebagai perbandingan.

item.title: JAKIM Budget Hits Record RM2.6 Billion — Highest in Malaysian History
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Finance Minister

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-10-11

Budget 2026 allocated RM2.6B for Islamic affairs — the highest in Malaysian history. Trajectory: RM1.9B (2024), RM2B (2025), RM2.6B (2026). Unity Ministry got only RM711M by comparison.

item.title: [BM] Kontroversi Penjenamaan Semula Tabung Haji — Dakwaan Perbelanjaan RM20J, Ketegangan Kaum Meningkat
item.description:
Said by: Tabung Haji Management

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-08-16

Tabung Haji’s rebranding exercise became a racial flashpoint when allegations emerged of RM20M spent with non-Muslim companies involved. TH denied, claiming only RM2M/year. MCMC investigated critics instead of auditing the procurement.

item.title: Tabung Haji Rebranding Controversy — Alleged RM20M Spend, Racial Tensions Flare
item.description:
Said by: Tabung Haji Management

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-08-16

Tabung Haji’s rebranding exercise became a racial flashpoint when allegations emerged of RM20M spent with non-Muslim companies involved. TH denied, claiming only RM2M/year. MCMC investigated critics instead of auditing the procurement.

item.title: [BM] Anwar Berpatah Balik Soal Garis Panduan Muslim Di Majlis Bukan Islam Selepas Tentangan Hebat
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-02-07

Cadang Perpecahan Dahulu, Kemudian Berlagak Tak Tahu

Pada awal Februari 2025, kerajaan Anwar mencadangkan garis panduan terbitan JAKIM yang menyekat penyertaan Muslim di majlis keagamaan bukan Islam. Tentangan datang serta-merta dan dahsyat. Dalam beberapa hari, Anwar membatalkan cadangan itu, berkata “tiada keperluan” kerana umat Islam “sudah sepatutnya tahu” peraturan.

Fakta

Fakta: Pada 5 Februari 2025, Menteri Hal Ehwal Agama Mohd Na’im Mokhtar mengumumkan garis panduan baru yang mewajibkan penganjur mendapat kebenaran pihak berkuasa Islam sebelum menjemput Muslim, melarang majlis berhampiran masjid, dan mengharamkan “propaganda agama.”

Fakta: Sultan Selangor Sharafuddin Idris Shah mengkritik garis panduan tersebut secara terbuka sebagai campur tangan yang tidak perlu.

Fakta: Pada 7 Februari 2025, Anwar membalikkan keputusan selepas mesyuarat Kabinet: “Tiada keperluan garis panduan sedemikian kerana umat Islam tahu peraturan agama kita.”

Masalahnya

  • Garis panduan ini bukan digubal oleh birokrat nakal. Ia datang daripada kementerian dalam kerajaan Anwar sendiri.
  • Pusingan U hanya berlaku selepas Sultan bersuara dan kemarahan awam memuncak. Tanpa tekanan itu, adakah garis panduan akan kekal?
  • Ini mendedahkan naluri kerajaan: mengampu konservatif agama dahulu, berundur apabila gagal. *Sumber: Straits Times · CNA · *SCMP

item.title: Anwar Backtracks on Muslim Guidelines for Non-Muslim Events After Fierce Backlash
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-02-07

First Propose Division, Then Pretend You Didn’t

In early February 2025, Anwar’s government proposed JAKIM-issued guidelines restricting Muslim participation at non-Muslim religious events. The backlash was immediate and ferocious. Within days, Anwar scrapped the proposal, saying “there is no need” because Muslims “should already know” the rules.

The Facts

Fact: On 5 February 2025, Religious Affairs Minister Mohd Na’im Mokhtar announced new guidelines requiring organisers to seek permission from Islamic authorities before inviting Muslims, banning events near mosques, and prohibiting “religious propaganda.”

Fact: Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah publicly criticised the guidelines as unnecessary interference.

Fact: On 7 February 2025, Anwar reversed the decision after the Cabinet meeting: “There is no need for such guidelines because Muslims know the rules of our faith.”

The Problem

  • The guidelines were not drafted by rogue bureaucrats. They came from a ministry within Anwar’s government.
  • The U-turn only came after the Sultan spoke and public anger mounted. Without that pressure, would the guidelines have stood?
  • This reveals the government’s instinct: pander to religious conservatives first, then retreat when it backfires. *Sources: Straits Times · CNA · *SCMP

item.title: [BM] Perkahwinan Kanak-Kanak MASIH Sah — PH Janji Hapuskannya, Kemudian Tidak Buat Apa Kerana 'Sensitiviti Islam'
item.description:
Said by: Sisters in Islam / UNICEF / Women’s Rights Groups

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Walaupun bertahun-tahun kempen oleh pertubuhan hak wanita dan janji jelas daripada ahli politik PH, perkahwinan kanak-kanak kekal sah di Malaysia. Di bawah undang-undang keluarga Islam (bidang kuasa negeri), gadis semuda 16 tahun boleh berkahwin dengan kelulusan Mahkamah Syariah, dan dalam praktiknya, perkahwinan yang lebih muda telah didokumentasikan.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Perkahwinan kanak-kanak merampas pendidikan, kemerdekaan ekonomi, dan autonomi tubuh gadis. Ia dikaitkan dengan kadar keganasan rumah tangga yang lebih tinggi, kemiskinan, komplikasi kesihatan daripada kehamilan awal, dan kelemahan antara generasi. UNICEF telah berulang kali menggesa Malaysia menaikkan umur minimum perkahwinan kepada 18 tahun untuk semua.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

PH mempunyai majoriti besar di parlimen dengan sokongan BN. Mereka mempunyai jumlah untuk menggubal perubahan. Mereka memilih untuk tidak berbuat demikian. Alasannya sentiasa ‘sensitiviti Islam’ dan ‘bidang kuasa negeri.’ Tetapi apabila PH mahukan sesuatu dilakukan — seperti Akta Keselamatan Dalam Talian — tiba-tiba penyelarasan negeri-persekutuan bukan halangan. Perkahwinan kanak-kanak berterusan kerana kos politik untuk menghapuskannya melebihi manfaat politik.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Pada 2018, TPM PH 1.0 Wan Azizah berjanji untuk menghapuskan perkahwinan kanak-kanak. Janji itu tidak ke mana. Menjelang 2020, PH 1.0 runtuh tanpa meluluskan sebarang undang-undang. PH 2.0 Anwar tidak pun membangkitkan isu ini secara bermakna. Janji itu telah dikitar semula merentasi dua kerajaan PH tanpa sebarang tindakan perundangan.

🎯 Seruan Terhadap PH

Kamu mempunyai jumlah parlimen. Kamu mempunyai hujah moral. Kamu mempunyai tekanan antarabangsa. Dan kamu MASIH tidak akan bertindak kerana kamu takut mengecewakan pengundi Melayu-Islam konservatif. Kamu telah membuat pengiraan politik bahawa masa depan gadis kecil bernilai kurang daripada margin undi. Itu bukan tadbir urus — itu pengecut.

Sumber: Malay Mail / Amnesty International

item.title: Child Marriage STILL Legal — PH Promised to End It, Then Did Nothing Because 'Islamic Sensitivities'
item.description:
Said by: Sisters in Islam / UNICEF / Women’s Rights Groups

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

Despite years of campaigning by women’s rights organisations and explicit promises from PH politicians, child marriage remains legal in Malaysia. Under Islamic family law (state jurisdiction), girls as young as 16 can be married with Shariah court approval, and in practice, younger marriages have been documented.

🔥 Why It Matters

Child marriage robs girls of education, economic independence, and bodily autonomy. It is associated with higher rates of domestic violence, poverty, health complications from early pregnancy, and intergenerational disadvantage. UNICEF has repeatedly urged Malaysia to raise the minimum marriage age to 18 for all.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH has a supermajority in parliament with BN support. They have the numbers to legislate change. They choose not to. The excuse is always ‘Islamic sensitivities’ and ‘state jurisdiction.’ But when PH wants something done — like the Online Safety Act — suddenly state-federal coordination isn’t a barrier. Child marriage persists because the political cost of ending it outweighs the political benefit.

📜 Previous Cases

In 2018, PH 1.0 Deputy PM Wan Azizah pledged to end child marriage. The pledge went nowhere. By 2020, PH 1.0 collapsed without passing any legislation. Anwar’s PH 2.0 hasn’t even raised the issue meaningfully. The promise has been recycled across two PH governments without any legislative action.

🎯 PH Callout

You have the parliamentary numbers. You have the moral argument. You have international pressure. And you STILL won’t act because you’re terrified of upsetting conservative Malay-Muslim voters. You’ve made a political calculation that little girls’ futures are worth less than vote margins. That’s not governance — that’s cowardice.

Sources

  • Malay Mail / Amnesty International — Reform Failures

item.title: [BM] Kontroversi Pensijilan Halal — Bukan Islam Terperangkap Dalam Pertembungan Keterlaluan Agama
item.description:
Said by: Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, DPM / Teresa Kok, MP

Category: Religion

Date: 2024-09-15

Halaman asal tidak mempunyai kandungan.

item.title: [BM] Kontroversi Pensijilan Halal — Bukan Islam Terperangkap Dalam Pertembungan Pencerobohan Agama
item.description:
Said by: Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, DPM / Teresa Kok, MP

Category: Religion

Date: 2024-09-15

Debate erupted over forcing non-Muslim businesses to get halal certification. Kelantan required it for premises serving Muslims. Teresa Kok argued it should remain voluntary. Zahid had to clarify no cabinet decision was made.

item.title: Halal Certification Controversy — Non-Muslims Caught in Crossfire of Religious Overreach
item.description:
Said by: Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, DPM / Teresa Kok, MP

Category: Religion

Date: 2024-09-15

Debate erupted over forcing non-Muslim businesses to get halal certification. Kelantan required it for premises serving Muslims. Teresa Kok argued it should remain voluntary. Zahid had to clarify no cabinet decision was made.

item.title: [BM] Stoking Allah KK Mart — Kedai Dibom Molotov Sementara Anwar Suruh Orang Ramai Jangan Jadi Hakim
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Religion

Date: 2024-03-25

Bom Api Dan Ceramah Tentang Kesabaran

Pada Mac 2024, stoking yang tertera perkataan “Allah” ditemui dijual di cawangan KK Super Mart. Apa yang menyusul adalah berminggu kemarahan, seruan boikot, dan tiga serangan bom Molotov ke atas kedai. Kerajaan Anwar mengambil beberapa hari untuk merespons. Apabila PM akhirnya bersuara, beliau memberitahu orang ramai jangan bertindak sebagai “pendakwa atau hakim.”

Fakta

Fakta: Pada 13 Mac 2024, stoking dengan perkataan “Allah” ditemui di KK Super Mart di Sunway City. Hanya 14 pasang ditemui di tiga cawangan.

Fakta: Tiga cawangan KK Mart diserang dengan bom Molotov di Perak, Pahang, dan Sarawak. Tiada kecederaan dilaporkan.

Fakta: Pemilik KK Mart dan pembekal didakwa atas “mencederakan perasaan agama.” Mereka mengaku tidak bersalah.

Fakta: Anwar, pada 25 Mac, memberitahu orang ramai: “Anda bukan pendakwa mahupun hakim… negara tidak akan boleh melihat apa-apa pertumbuhan jika rakyat memutuskan untuk terus membangkitkan isu tertentu.”

Kegagalan Kerajaan

  • Respons yang lemah itulah ceritanya. Pengkritik mencatatkan Anwar dan Timbalan PM Zahid lambat menangani krisis, membenarkannya menjadi keganasan vigilante.
  • Memberitahu orang ramai untuk bertenang selepas bom petrol dilemparkan ke kedai bukan kepimpinan — ia kawalan kerosakan.
  • Stoking itu adalah kesilapan pembekal. Keganasan itu adalah kegagalan tadbir urus. *Sumber: The Guardian · Straits Times · BBC · *SCMP

item.title: KK Mart Allah Socks — Stores Firebombed While Anwar Tells Public Not to Be Judge
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Religion

Date: 2024-03-25

Firebombs and a Lecture on Patience

In March 2024, socks bearing the word “Allah” were found for sale at KK Super Mart outlets. What followed was weeks of outrage, boycott calls, and three Molotov cocktail attacks on stores. Anwar’s government took days to respond. When the PM finally spoke, he told the public not to act as “prosecutor or judge.”

The Facts

Fact: On 13 March 2024, socks with the word “Allah” were discovered at a KK Super Mart in Sunway City. Only 14 pairs were found across three outlets.

Fact: Three KK Mart outlets were attacked with Molotov cocktails in Perak, Pahang, and Sarawak. No injuries were reported.

Fact: KK Mart’s owners and the supplier were charged with “wounding religious feelings.” They pleaded not guilty.

Fact: Anwar, on 25 March, told the public: “You’re neither prosecutor nor judge… the country would not be able to see any growth if the people decided to harp on certain issues.”

The Government’s Failure

  • The feeble response was the story. Critics noted Anwar and Deputy PM Zahid were slow to address the crisis, allowing it to spiral into vigilante violence.
  • Telling the public to calm down after petrol bombs have been thrown at shops is not leadership — it is damage control.
  • The socks were a supplier error. The violence was a governance failure. *Sources: The Guardian · Straits Times · BBC · *SCMP

item.title: [BM] Keputusan Mahkamah Persekutuan Mengenai Undang-Undang Syariah Kelantan — PH Meraikan, Kemudian Senyap Soal Reformasi
item.description:
Said by: Federal Court of Malaysia

Category: Religion

Date: 2024-02-09

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Februari 2024, Mahkamah Persekutuan membatalkan 16 daripada 18 peruntukan yang dicabar dalam Kanun Jenayah Syariah Kelantan sebagai tidak berperlembagaan, memutuskan bahawa negeri yang ditadbir PAS itu telah melampaui kuasa perundangannya. Pemimpin PH pada awalnya meraikan keputusan itu sebagai kemenangan bagi keluhuran perlembagaan. Kemudian kerajaan senyap sepenuhnya mengenai reformasi yang lebih luas. Walaupun keputusan itu mewujudkan asas undang-undang yang jelas untuk menangani pencerobohan serupa di negeri lain, tiada tindakan perundangan diambil. Pelbagai negeri mempunyai peruntukan setanding yang kekal tidak disentuh.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Keputusan bersejarah Mahkamah Persekutuan menyerahkan kepada PH asas undang-undang yang sempurna untuk memajukan reformasi Syariah dalam sempadan perlembagaan — dan mereka tidak melakukan apa-apa dengannya. Keputusan itu menetapkan bahawa badan perundangan negeri tidak boleh menggubal undang-undang Syariah yang bertindih dengan bidang kuasa jenayah persekutuan. Prinsip ini terpakai kepada pelbagai negeri, bukan sahaja Kelantan. Tanpa susulan perundangan, keputusan itu kekal sebagai peristiwa undang-undang terpencil bukannya reformasi sistemik. PH mempunyai preseden undang-undang, bilangan parlimen, dan kuasa perlembagaan untuk bertindak. Mereka memilih senyap.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Kesunyian PH adalah strategik: menyentuh reformasi Syariah berisiko kehilangan undi Melayu. Tetapi itulah tepat-tepat makna reformasi — melakukan perkara sukar yang membawa risiko politik. Kerajaan yang hanya mereformasi apabila ia selamat secara politik tidak mereformasi langsung — ia mengurus optik. PH meraikan keputusan itu secara terbuka, menggunakannya untuk satu kitaran berita liputan “kami menyokong keluhuran perlembagaan,” dan kemudian menanamnya. Tiada perundangan susulan. Tiada semakan peruntukan serupa di negeri lain. Tiada komitmen awam untuk memastikan sempadan perlembagaan dikuatkuasakan di seluruh negara.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Ketegangan antara bidang kuasa Syariah persekutuan dan negeri telah menjadi isu perlembagaan sejak pembentukan Malaysia. PAS secara konsisten mendorong sempadan kuasa Syariah negeri — percubaan “rang undang-undang hudud” di Parlimen, pengembangan bidang kuasa mahkamah Syariah di Kelantan dan Terengganu, dan penggunaan pihak berkuasa agama negeri untuk mempoliskan tingkah laku moral. Setiap pemimpin PH telah mengkritik pencerobohan ini dalam pembangkang. Dalam kerajaan, mereka meraikan kemenangan mahkamah tetapi enggan membuat susulan dengan perundangan — kerana kos politik dilihat sebagai “anti-Islam” dianggap terlalu tinggi.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Mahkamah Persekutuan memberikan anda asas undang-undang untuk reformasi Syariah atas dulang perak. Anda meraikan sehari dan senyap selama dua tahun. Semak dan cabar peruntukan serupa di setiap negeri. Tetapkan sempadan perundangan yang jelas antara bidang kuasa Syariah persekutuan dan negeri. Gunakan keputusan itu sebagai asas pakej reformasi perlembagaan. Atau terus meraikan kemenangan mahkamah yang anda tiada niat untuk membina — dan biarkan negeri yang ditadbir PAS seterusnya menguji sempadan yang sama sekali lagi.

Sumber: Straits Times | Malaysiakini

item.title: Federal Court Ruling on Kelantan Shariah Laws — PH Celebrates, Then Goes Quiet on Reform
item.description:
Said by: Federal Court of Malaysia

Category: Religion

Date: 2024-02-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In February 2024, the Federal Court struck down 16 out of 18 challenged provisions in Kelantan’s Shariah Criminal Code as unconstitutional, ruling that the PAS-governed state had overstepped its legislative authority. PH leaders initially celebrated the ruling as a victory for constitutional supremacy. Then the government went completely silent on broader reform. Despite the ruling creating a clear legal foundation to address similar overreach in other states, no legislative action was taken. Multiple states have comparable provisions that remain untouched.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: A landmark Federal Court ruling handed PH a perfect legal foundation to advance Shariah reform within constitutional bounds — and they did nothing with it. The ruling established that state legislatures cannot enact Shariah laws that overlap with federal criminal jurisdiction. This principle applies to multiple states, not just Kelantan. Without legislative follow-up, the ruling remains an isolated legal event rather than systemic reform. PH had the legal precedent, the parliamentary numbers, and the constitutional authority to act. They chose silence.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH’s silence is strategic: touching Shariah reform risks losing Malay votes. But that is exactly what reform means — doing difficult things that carry political risk. A government that only reforms when it’s politically safe isn’t reforming at all — it’s managing optics. PH celebrated the ruling publicly, used it for one news cycle of “we support constitutional supremacy” coverage, and then buried it. No follow-up legislation. No review of similar provisions in other states. No public commitment to ensure constitutional boundaries are enforced nationwide.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The tension between federal and state Shariah jurisdiction has been a constitutional issue since Malaysia’s formation. PAS has consistently pushed the boundaries of state Shariah authority — the “hudud bill” attempts in Parliament, the expansion of Shariah court jurisdiction in Kelantan and Terengganu, and the use of state religious authorities to police moral behaviour. Every PH leader has criticised these overreaches in opposition. In government, they celebrate court victories but refuse to follow through with legislation — because the political cost of being seen as “anti-Islam” is considered too high.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: The Federal Court gave you a legal foundation for Shariah reform on a silver platter. You celebrated for a day and went silent for two years. Review and challenge similar provisions in every state. Establish clear legislative boundaries between federal and state Shariah jurisdiction. Use the ruling as the basis for a constitutional reform package. Or keep celebrating court victories you have no intention of building on — and let the next PAS-governed state test the same boundaries all over again.

Sources: Straits Times | Malaysiakini
